_comment: Product capability content for the public site. Present what the engine does, confidently; keep the granular maturity/limitations nuance in docs/CAPABILITY.md, not here. 'proof' is an optional positive credibility signal shown only where genuinely true. 'run' loads a live scenario in the playground.

capabilities.name: Orbit propagation & constellation design
capabilities.summary: SGP4/SDP4 propagation from real two-line elements (a committed Celestrak GPS snapshot) or synthetic Walker constellations whose mean elements realise the i:T/P/F formula to under 1 km over 24 h. Multi-constellation visibility, dilution of precision and GNSS availability, a constellation-design optimiser, streets-of-coverage sizing, and a Walker design sweep that reports the Pareto-optimal designs over a planes × satellites grid.

capabilities.name: Time scales & Earth-orientation frames
capabilities.summary: IERS leap-second UTC / TAI / TT / UT1 scales, a Julian-date API, the IAU-2000 Earth Rotation Angle, GMST-based TEME ↔ ECEF and WGS-84 geodetic frames, and IAU 2006 precession toward an ITRF-precise reduction.

capabilities.name: Strapdown INS & map-aided navigation
capabilities.summary: Three-axis strapdown INS — quaternion attitude, WGS-84 NED mechanization, coning/sculling compensation, and a full deterministic IMU error model — plus a first-principles cold-atom-interferometer accelerometer (Mach–Zehnder phase, projection noise, contrast decay, vibration coupling) and a particle filter for terrain-/gravity-referenced GPS-denied navigation.

capabilities.name: GNSS / INS sensor fusion
capabilities.summary: A 15-state error-state EKF that coasts through GNSS outages on a calibrated inertial solution; a tightly-coupled pseudorange mode that keeps correcting with fewer than four satellites; a coupled clock + position filter; a general unscented (sigma-point) Kalman estimator; and a tightly-coupled GNSS/INS UKF navigator validated to 0.77 m RMS through a 120 s outage on a curving LEO pass.

capabilities.name: Receiver autonomous integrity (ARAIM)
capabilities.summary: Snapshot and solution-separation RAIM with horizontal and vertical protection levels (HPL/VPL), fault detection and exclusion, and Stanford integrity diagrams — plus an explicit integrity-risk-budget (MHSS) protection level with the dual-/multi-constellation constellation-wide fault mode (EU ARAIM / DO-316).

capabilities.name: Holdover, stability & time-and-frequency transfer
capabilities.summary: Two-state Kalman holdover, Allan-family stability (ADEV / MDEV / TDEV / HDEV) with confidence intervals and a full IEEE-1139 five-coefficient power-law fit, and the operational transfer methods — TWSTFT with the BIPM Sagnac closed form, GNSS common-view, PPP ionosphere-free transfer, a free-space optical link with turbulence scintillation, and an inverse-variance clock-ensemble timescale.

capabilities.name: Pseudorange, ionosphere & troposphere
capabilities.summary: Forward pseudorange / Doppler synthesis with Klobuchar (broadcast) and IONEX / TEC-grid (measured) ionosphere — including an IONEX file parser, time interpolation, and the thin-shell slant-obliquity mapping — Saastamoinen + Niell troposphere, and snapshot RAIM.

capabilities.name: Jamming & multi-layer spoof detection
capabilities.summary: A link-budget jamming model (J/S → effective C/N₀ → loss of lock), a stochastic time-spoof detector (Neyman–Pearson / χ²₁ energy test with Monte-Carlo P_fa/P_md and a Security FoM of 1 − P_md), and a multi-layer spoof detector fusing a RAIM-consistency parity test, an RF AGC-power monitor, and a signal-quality (SQM) monitor.

capabilities.name: RINEX, SP3 & CCSDS formats
capabilities.summary: Multi-GNSS RINEX-3 broadcast-ephemeris ingestion (GPS, Galileo, QZSS, BeiDou, GLONASS), a RINEX-3/4 observation parser, an SP3-c/d precise-ephemeris reader/writer with Lagrange interpolation, and CCSDS OEM 2.0 + OMM (mean-elements) export for flight-dynamics tools (GMAT, Orekit, STK).

trust.reproducible: Deterministic from scenario + seed + engine version; CycloneDX SBOM and SLSA build provenance on every release.
trust.citation: CITATION.cff in the repository; archival DOI via Zenodo.
